title: Ukraine Drone Barrage Sets Fire at Russia's Primorsk Baltic Oil Port, Hits Missile Ship
summary: Over 60 Ukrainian drones targeted Russia's Leningrad region overnight, striking the Primorsk oil export port on the Baltic Sea and hitting a Karakurt-class missile ship, patrol boat, and shadow fleet tanker. The port handles up to 1 million barrels per day.
contents:
## Major Ukrainian Drone Campaign Targets Primorsk Oil Hub

### Attack Scale
Ukrainian forces launched a massive drone barrage overnight into May 3, with over 60 drones targeting Russia's northwestern Leningrad region alone. The strikes set fire at the port of Primorsk, Russia's largest oil exporting facility on the Baltic Sea, capable of handling up to one million barrels per day.

### Damage Assessment
Leningrad Governor Alexander Drozdenko confirmed the fire at Primorsk but stated it resulted in no oil spill and crews extinguished the blaze. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy claimed significant damage to oil port infrastructure and the destruction of multiple military and civilian targets.

### Naval Targets
Zelenskyy reported Ukrainian drones struck a Karakurt-class missile ship ("one more Russian carrier of Kalibr missiles out of action"), a patrol boat, and a tanker belonging to Russia's shadow oil fleet near the port. The operation targeted vessels supporting both Russia's naval warfare capability and sanctions-evading oil exports.

### Distance and Capability
Primorsk sits over 1,000 kilometers (620 miles) from Ukraine, between the Russian-Finnish border and St. Petersburg. The successful strike at this range demonstrates Ukraine's expanding long-range drone capability.

### Russian Casualties Elsewhere
A Ukrainian drone strike west of Moscow killed a 77-year-old man near Volokolamsk, approximately 120 kilometers from central Moscow. Three people, including a child, suffered injuries when a drone hit an apartment block in the Smolensk region.

**Sources:** AP, Reuters, Bloomberg
